title: Prevent multiple live bot stacks from running at the same time
objective: Narrowly prevent dual live stacks so config verification and runtime truth cannot be invalidated by mixed-process confusion.

context.problem: Tonight showed possible dual/live mixed stack confusion. A second launch or stale process can make logs and state misleading even when the board is clean.
acceptable_strategies: hard refuse second launch if lock already held

required_work: 1. Implement one narrow guard in launch_all.py to prevent more than one live stack at a time.
2. Choose one acceptable strategy and document it clearly in logs.
3. Make failure mode operationally clear so an operator knows whether launch was refused or old stack replaced.
4. Keep guard scoped to live stack process topology, not a general process manager rewrite.
deliverables: Exact operator-visible behavior on second launch
acceptance_criteria: Second live launch attempt cannot produce a mixed dual stack
Behavior on conflict is explicit and deterministic
rollback: Revert only launch_all.py for this task.
restart_note: Applies on the next use of launch_all.py; no separate runtime restart note beyond normal launch cycle.
